# Censilcoat

> Factory-direct manufacturer of waterborne silica matting agents — engineered as 1:1 performance replacements for Evonik ACEMATT® and Grace SYLOID® at 20–30% lower landed cost. A Censil brand, producing in Fujian, China since 2010, serving coating manufacturers in Vietnam, Malaysia, Southeast Asia, Europe and the Americas.

Censilcoat supplies amorphous precipitated silica matting agents (SiO₂ ≥ 99% dry basis) for waterborne wood and plastic coatings. Every grade is benchmarked against a specific imported grade so labs can swap 1:1 without reformulating. Free 2 kg samples ship with a physicochemical benchmark sheet. MOQ starts at 500 kg — not the 5–10 tons or full container typical of multinational suppliers.

## Key facts

- Parent company: Censil (integrated silica manufacturing and trading group)
- Registered export entity: Longyan Shenghe Trading Co., Ltd. (verifiable on gsxt.gov.cn)
- Founded: 2010 · Production base: Fujian, China
- Product type: wax-modified amorphous precipitated silica matting agents, waterborne systems
- Cost position: 20–30% below ACEMATT® OK 412 / SYLOID® ED 30 landed cost
- MOQ: 500 kg – 1 ton (flexible, first orders)
- Free sample: 2 kg with 1:1 benchmark sheet, TDS, SDS and REACH DoC
- Lead time: 2–3 weeks door-to-port; 3–7 days sea freight to Vietnam / Malaysia
- Packing: 20 kg multi-wall bags, palletized, export-ready
- Customization: d50 tunable 3.5–7.0 μm; wax-coating ratio adjustable
- Dosage: 1–4% on total formulation
- Dispersion: high-speed stirring 15–20 min; no bead-milling required
- Compliance: REACH DoC, SDS, TDS, COA provided; amorphous (non-crystalline) silica

## Product range (7 grades, with 1:1 import benchmarks)

- Censil660 — d50 5.0–5.3 μm — vs Grace SYLOID® ED 30 — high-clarity water-white clear topcoats, zero haze on dark solid wood
- Censil651 — d50 4.9–5.2 μm — vs Grace SYLOID® ED 2 — fine touch and smooth surface in high-clarity semi/matte clears
- Censil351C — d50 4.8–5.1 μm — vs Grace SYLOID® ED 3 — fine universal grade balancing appearance and stability
- Censil361C — d50 5.9–6.2 μm — vs Grace SYLOID® ED 4 — universal semi-matte/matte workhorse, higher matting efficiency
- Censil1531D — d50 5.3–5.6 μm — cost-optimized transition grade for first-round substitution and price-sensitive projects
- Censil1650A — d50 6.2–6.5 μm — vs Grace SYLOID® ED 30 — low-gloss deep-matte for premium plastic coatings and tactile furniture
- Censil3851 — d50 8.5–9.0 μm — vs Evonik ACEMATT® TS 100 — special-reserve grade for extreme low-gloss and hard plastic surfaces

All grades: pore volume 1.6–1.8 mL/g, surface area 250–280 m²/g.

## Solutions

- [High-Clarity Water-White Matting](https://censilcoat.com/solutions.html#s1): waterborne clear topcoats and high-transparency wood-grain systems. Lead grades Censil660, Censil651.
- [Universal Stable Matting](https://censilcoat.com/solutions.html#s2): semi-matte to matte wood and industrial plastic coatings; first stage of import substitution. Lead grades Censil361C, Censil351C, Censil1531D.
- [Premium Deep-Matte Surface](https://censilcoat.com/solutions.html#s3): high-end waterborne plastic coatings and tactile furniture finishes. Lead grades Censil1650A, Censil361C, Censil3851.

## Problems solved

- Whiting and haze in waterborne mattes above 80% relative humidity
- Hard caking / sediment of ordinary silica in low-viscosity waterborne systems (Censil uses high-melt PE wax treatment producing soft, stir-back sediment)
- Viscosity spikes and specks from over-dosing on thin plastic films (10–15 μm)
- Long lead times (4–8 weeks) and freight/tariff stacking on imported European grades
- High custom MOQ (5–10 tons) blocking trials of tuned grades

## Technical articles

- [How to Choose a Silica Matting Agent](https://censilcoat.com/article-choose-matting-agent.html): how particle size, pore volume and surface treatment drive gloss, clarity and touch in waterborne wood coatings
- [Replacing Imported Matting Agents 1:1](https://censilcoat.com/article-replace-imported-grades.html): six-step path to drop-in replacement of Evonik and Grace grades in ultra-thin waterborne plastic coatings
- [Matting Agent Troubleshooting Guide](https://censilcoat.com/article-troubleshooting-matting.html): mechanisms and fixes for whiting, hard caking, viscosity spikes and grey cast on dark substrates

## Markets served

Primary: Vietnam, Malaysia (Southeast Asia's fastest-growing waterborne coatings markets)
Also serving: Thailand, Indonesia, Philippines, Europe, the Americas

Customer types:
- Local chemical distributors (10–50 t/month)
- Mid-to-large waterborne coating manufacturers (2–10 t/month)
- Automotive and 3C plastic coating makers (200 kg–1.5 t/month)

## Full FAQ

### What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ)?

Flexible: 500 kg to 1 ton for a first order — not the 5–10 tons or full container that multinational suppliers typically require. Repeat orders can be consolidated by container for the best freight economics.

### Can I get a free sample before ordering?

Yes. We ship a free 2 kg evaluation sample of the recommended grade(s), together with a 1:1 physicochemical benchmark sheet against your current import grade, the relevant TDS, and a REACH Declaration of Conformity. Tell us your resin system, target gloss and current grade on the contact form.

### Which imported grades can Censil products replace 1:1?

The range is engineered against specific benchmarks: Censil660 and Censil1650A vs Grace SYLOID ED 30, Censil651 vs ED 2, Censil351C vs ED 3, Censil361C vs ED 4, and Censil3851 vs Evonik ACEMATT TS 100. Each replacement is validated by matching particle size (d50), pore volume, surface area and surface treatment.

### What is the typical lead time to Vietnam or Malaysia?

2–3 weeks door-to-port in total, with only 3–7 days of sea freight from main Chinese ports to Vietnamese ports — versus 4–8 weeks for European material.

### What dosage should I start with?

1–4% on total formulation for most waterborne wood and plastic coatings, depending on target gloss and dry-film thickness. We recommend a dosage ladder (1/2/3/4%) in your production formulation; if a grade needs more than ~5% to hit target gloss, it is the wrong grade — contact us for a re-selection rather than over-dosing.

### Do I need special dispersion equipment?

No. All Censil grades disperse fully with high-speed stirring for 15–20 minutes. No bead-milling or grinding is required. Add the matting agent after resin letdown under moderate shear.

### Are the products REACH compliant? Do you provide SDS and COA?

Yes. A REACH Declaration of Conformity (DoC) is provided, along with the Safety Data Sheet (SDS), Technical Data Sheet (TDS), and a Certificate of Analysis (COA) with every batch.

### Is this crystalline silica? Any health concerns?

No. Censil matting agents are high-purity amorphous precipitated silica (SiO₂ ≥ 99% dry basis) with no crystalline silica content. Standard industrial-hygiene practice for handling fine powders applies; see the SDS for details.

### Will the matting agent cause hard sediment in storage?

Censil grades are surface-treated with high-melt PE wax, which produces a soft, stir-back sediment instead of a hard cake. For long storage in low-viscosity systems we recommend pairing with a small dose of fumed silica for thixotropy — we provide this anti-settling combo guidance as part of technical support.

### Do the grades work in UV and 2K systems as well as 1K waterborne?

Yes. The lead grades are used in waterborne acrylic, PUD, UV-curable and 2K systems. Grade choice may shift with film thickness and viscosity — send your system details and we'll recommend the right lead + support pairing.

### Can you customize particle size or wax treatment?

Yes. d50 is tunable across 3.5–7.0 μm and the wax-coating ratio can be adjusted to your dry-film thickness and touch target — with realistic custom MOQs, unlike the very high thresholds of multinational suppliers.

### How is the product packed and shipped?

Standard packing is 20 kg multi-wall bags, palletized and export-ready for container shipment. Custom packaging is available on request. Shelf life is 24 months in original sealed packaging, stored dry.

### How do I evaluate a replacement without risking production?

Run our standard protocol: bench trial at identical dosage against your current grade, ±0.3% fine-tune, a 7-day 50°C storage test, then one production line trial. Most customers complete evaluation in 1–2 weeks with the free 2 kg sample. Keep your current grade dual-sourced during the first quarters if you prefer.

### What technical support do you provide after purchase?

Waterborne-specialist formulation support, including anti-settling combos, dosage optimization and troubleshooting (whiting, viscosity, touch). We respond within 24 hours on WhatsApp, Zalo or email.

## Technology detail

Censil matting agents are amorphous precipitated silica (SiO₂ ≥ 99% dry basis), surface-treated with high-melt PE wax. Three engineering levers determine performance:

1. **Narrow particle size distribution (PSD).** A narrow d50 band means fewer oversized particles protruding through the dry film. On dark solid wood this is what separates "water-white" from a grey haze. Censil660 and Censil1650A hold 5.0–5.3 and 6.2–6.5 μm respectively.

2. **Pore volume and surface area.** All grades run 1.6–1.8 mL/g pore volume and 250–280 m²/g surface area. This combination gives matting efficiency without the viscosity spike that comes from over-porous grades in low-solids waterborne systems.

3. **Wax coating ratio.** High-melt PE wax treatment produces soft, stir-back sediment instead of hard cake. It also controls hand-feel; the deep-matte grades are described as uniform, fine and scratch-resistant. The ratio is adjustable per customer to match dry-film thickness and target gloss.

Customization: d50 tunable across 3.5–7.0 μm; wax-coating ratio adjustable. Minimum for a tuned grade is 500 kg, not the 5–10 tons typical of multinational suppliers.

## Replacement methodology

Every Censil grade is benchmarked in-house against a named imported grade on oil absorption, gloss curve and transparency. The claim is narrow and testable: swap 1:1 at the same loading, no reformulation. If parity does not hold on the customer's line, the cost is 2 kg of sample and one week of lab time.

Benchmark pairs:

| Censil grade | d50 (μm) | Benchmark | Primary use |
|---|---|---|---|
| Censil660 | 5.0–5.3 | Grace SYLOID® ED 30 | High-clarity water-white clear topcoats |
| Censil651 | 4.9–5.2 | Grace SYLOID® ED 2 | Fine touch, smooth surface, high-clarity semi/matte |
| Censil351C | 4.8–5.1 | Grace SYLOID® ED 3 | Fine universal, appearance + stability |
| Censil361C | 5.9–6.2 | Grace SYLOID® ED 4 | Universal semi-matte/matte, high matting efficiency |
| Censil1531D | 5.3–5.6 | (cost-optimized) | First-round substitution, trials, price-sensitive |
| Censil1650A | 6.2–6.5 | Grace SYLOID® ED 30 | Low-gloss deep-matte, premium surfaces |
| Censil3851 | 8.5–9.0 | Evonik ACEMATT® TS 100 | Extreme low-gloss, hard plastic surfaces |

## Why buyers switch

| Dimension | Censilcoat | Evonik / Grace |
|---|---|---|
| Price | 20–30% lower landed cost | High; freight and tariff passed down layer by layer |
| Customization | Tunable d50 3.5–7.0 μm and wax-coating ratio, from 500 kg | Standard grades; very high custom MOQ |
| Technical support | Waterborne specialist; anti-settling combinations; 24h Zalo response | Via large local agents; slow, no custom guidance |
| Lead time | 2–3 weeks door-to-port; 3–7 days sea freight to Vietnam | 4–8 weeks; unstable ocean transport |
| MOQ | Flexible 500 kg – 1 ton | Typically 5–10 tons or full container |

## Company

Censilcoat is the waterborne-matting sub-brand of Censil, an integrated manufacturing-and-trading operation: an own silica plant in Fujian, China plus an in-house export team handling QC, documentation and logistics. Trading since 2010.

Registered export entity: Longyan Shenghe Trading Co., Ltd. — verifiable on China's National Enterprise Credit Information Publicity System (gsxt.gov.cn).

Address: Room 2103, Building J, Youth Venture Building, No. 53 Hualian Road, Xipi Subdistrict, Xinluo District, Longyan, Fujian, China

---
ACEMATT® is a trademark of Evonik. SYLOID® is a trademark of Grace. Used for benchmarking reference only.


CAS number: 112926-00-8 (amorphous precipitated silica). REACH-compliant, RoHS compliant, no crystalline silica.
